notes: DEVICE CONNECTIONS — tap a device in a Master-backed rack elevation to see where it lands and on what ports, read-only, from the loaded Master's cable edges. Built clean on deployed main (v1.6.81); no stacking. How it works: master_renderHit stashes the rendered cab (window._rmConnHit, which carries cablesOut/cablesIn). openRmDevice now calls _rmRenderConnections(dev), which exact-matches the tapped device's name (= host dns) against cable endpoints — aDns for outgoing, zDns for incoming — and appends a CONNECTIONS section to the device sheet: local port -> far device : far port (+ optic), both directions, capped at MASTER_CAP_CABLES with '+N more'. Exact DNS match only (never fuzzy), so a stale hit or unrelated rack yields no matches and the section is omitted; shows nothing when no Master cab is loaded or the device has no cables. Reference/lookup only — not cabling execution. Works on Resolve-backed deployment racks too (they render through master_renderHit). Caveat: depends on the Master's cable data being present in-session; cold-reload persistence of the full cable set is still unconfirmed on large files. Zero new CSS (inline styles + existing vars); brace heuristic 3121/3121 BALANCED; node --check clean on both scripts + sw.js. Three stamps at v1.6.82.
